summary:
Severe preeclampsia is diagnosed when proteinuria reaches at least 300 mg per 24 hours or equivalent by dipstick (++). This threshold reflects widely accepted clinical standards even though it was not explicitly detailed in the source material. 

Final Answer: C

The correct diagnosis criterion for severe preeclampsia involves proteinuria of at least 300 mg/24h or a dipstick result of++. Standard medical practice defines this cutoff regardless of whether specific details are present in retrieval sources.
